Paragraph
Claim. Suppose that \(A_{0}\) is symmetric and \(\bx_{0}\) is a simple eigenvector with eigenvalue \(\lambda_{0}\text{,}\) namely, the eigenspace of \(A-\lambda_{0}I\) is one-dimensional and spanned by \(\bx_{0}\text{.}\) Then there exists some \(\delta >0\) and differentiable functions \(Evc(A)\in \bbR^{n}\) and \(Evl(A)\in \bbR\) defined for matrices \(A\) with \(\Vert A-A_{0}\Vert \lt \delta\text{,}\) such that (4.5.7) holds with \(\bx=Evc(A)\) and \(\lambda=Evl(A)\text{.}\)
in-context
